Title: Reconcile tab
Summary: Boot time .tres to JSON rebuild status. Per domain counts and per file errors.
Body:
## What runs

On every server start, after the port is open, an orchestrator runs once over the whole Godot project and rewrites every JSON in `data/<domain>/` from the matching `.tres`. This catches any edits made in Godot while Bleepforge was off.

The pass is cheap (about 60 ms on a corpus of 90 `.tres` files), idempotent, and runs before the live watcher starts so we do not double process churn during startup.

## What this tab shows

Per domain breakdown of the last reconcile:

- imported: count of `.tres` successfully imported into JSON.
- skipped: count of `.tres` skipped (wrong script_class, missing required field, etc.) with the file path and reason for each.
- errors: count of `.tres` that threw during import. File path and error message for each.

The diagnostics icon goes amber if any domain has skipped > 0, red if any has errors > 0.

## Common causes of errors

- A `.tres` with `Slug = ""` or `Id = ""` (mappers refuse the empty primary key).
- A `.tres` whose script_class does not match what the mapper expects.
- A parser regression on a malformed `.tres` (rare; the round trip parser is byte exact across the corpus).

## Forcing a rebuild

Restart the server. There used to be a manual "rebuild now" button here; it was removed once the automatic paths were trustworthy. To reseed everything from `.tres`, kill the server and start it again.
